prompt: I have a static CSV file of inventory data that we manually update weekly. Turn it into a self-updating file that pulls from our warehouse API every night, reconciles differences, and flags discrepancies for review.

Our team has a YAML config file for feature flags that developers edit by hand. I want it to become a living document that validates itself on every commit, alerts when flags are orphaned, and auto-generates documentation from its contents.

We have a static README.md in our repo. Make it self-updating: it should automatically reflect the current test coverage, latest release version, dependency status, and contributor count from live sources.

Our runbook is a static Notion page that's always out of date. Design a system where the runbook automatically updates its 'current status' sections by pulling from our monitoring tools and deployment system.

I have a JSON file that maps error codes to human-readable messages. It's manually maintained and constantly falls behind. Animate it so it auto-discovers new error codes from the codebase and flags unmapped ones.

Our team's on-call rotation schedule is a Google Sheet. Turn it into a living document that syncs with PagerDuty, highlights upcoming shifts, auto-detects scheduling conflicts, and sends reminders 24 hours before a shift.

We have a static database seed file for our test environment. Make it alive — it should regenerate itself from production data schemas weekly, sanitize PII, maintain referential integrity, and version itself in git.

Our architecture diagram is a static PNG that's perpetually stale. Can we animate it — make it auto-generate from actual service discovery, deployment manifests, and API gateway configs, rebuilding itself whenever the topology changes?

I have a changelog file that developers always forget to update. Make it self-maintaining: it should auto-populate from merged PR titles, categorize entries by type (feature, bugfix, chore), and format itself following Keep a Changelog conventions.

Our .env.example file is always missing variables that exist in the real .env. Make it a living artifact that monitors the codebase for environment variable references and flags any that aren't documented in the example file.
